name: Lysosomal restoration bootstrap for June 1, 2026
summary: Submitted bootstrap bundle establishing the first public baseline candidate for the lysosomal restoration track.

scope.question: What is the current human-relevant evidence landscape for Lysosomal Restoration, and what belongs in the first public baseline?

proposed_changes.summary: Add LAMP-2A restoration in aging liver source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source anchors the most direct mammalian aging evidence for restoring lysosomal degradation capacity.

proposed_changes.summary: Add C. elegans lysosomal-function aging source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supports lysosomal function as aging-relevant and required for multiple worm longevity pathways.

proposed_changes.summary: Add 2025 lysosomal surveillance response source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source adds current animal-healthspan evidence for a lysosome-centered adaptive response.

proposed_changes.summary: Add trehalose/TFEB lysosomal-clearance source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supports pharmacological tractability of TFEB-mediated lysosomal clearance while retaining disease-model limits.

proposed_changes.summary: Add open-label ambroxol Parkinson disease source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supplies the first human lysosomal target-engagement support through GCase biology.

proposed_changes.summary: Add randomized ambroxol Parkinson disease dementia source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supports human biomarker signal while limiting functional-benefit claims.

proposed_changes.summary: Add ASPro-PD phase 3 protocol source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source documents active clinical momentum without treating protocol status as efficacy evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged mouse LAMP-2A/CMA study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record captures the direct mammalian aging signal.

proposed_changes.summary: Add C. elegans lysosomal-function aging study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record captures age-related lysosomal decline and longevity-pathway dependence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add lysosomal surveillance response worm study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record captures the recent v-ATPase/LySR animal-healthspan signal.

proposed_changes.summary: Add trehalose/TFEB Batten disease model study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record captures pharmacological lysosomal-clearance tractability and disease-model limits.

proposed_changes.summary: Add open-label ambroxol Parkinson disease study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record anchors early human target-engagement evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add randomized ambroxol Parkinson disease dementia study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record captures the controlled human target-engagement signal and null functional boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Add ASPro-PD phase 3 study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record captures clinical-program momentum without outcome overreach.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged mouse LAMP-2A functional finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports animal aging relevance for restoring lysosomal degradation capacity.

proposed_changes.summary: Add C. elegans lysosomal-aging finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports mechanistic aging relevance and lifespan-pathway dependence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add lysosomal surveillance response finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding adds current animal-healthspan momentum with explicit translation limits.

proposed_changes.summary: Add trehalose/TFEB storage-disease finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports pharmacological tractability while preserving disease-model boundaries.

proposed_changes.summary: Add open-label ambroxol human biomarker finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding helps justify the human_biomarker_signal stage assignment.

proposed_changes.summary: Add randomized ambroxol mixed human biomarker finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports human target engagement and blocks a functional-benefit upgrade.

proposed_changes.summary: Add ASPro-PD phase 3 program-status finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports clinical momentum while avoiding efficacy overclaiming.

proposed_changes.summary: Create the first public outlook for the lysosomal restoration track.
proposed_changes.rationale: A low-confidence human_biomarker_signal baseline is justified by human ambroxol target engagement, while animal aging evidence and disease-model tractability support the track without establishing human functional benefit.
uncertainty_flags: No direct healthy-aging or broad geroscience human trial was identified.
Human evidence is disease-specific and centered on Parkinson disease GCase biology.
The randomized Parkinson disease dementia trial did not confirm cognitive benefit.
Preclinical signals are heterogeneous and include genetic, disease-model, and stress-response mechanisms.
Chronic lysosomal or autophagy-lysosome activation safety remains unresolved.

proposed_outlook_implications.note: A first public baseline is justified at low-confidence human_biomarker_signal because ambroxol studies show human lysosomal target engagement, while functional benefit and broad aging relevance remain unproven.
Publishing this bundle would broaden disabled macroautophagy coverage beyond autophagy induction and add a distinct lysosome-focused baseline.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
